basics.name: Junxiang (Jim) Wang | 王俊翔
basics.label: Masters Student
basics.summary: I am a Masters student majoring in Robotics at Johns Hopkins University, currently applying for Ph.D. programs. I obtained my B.S. in Mechanical Engineering from JHU in 2023.

work.summary: I have been serving as a teaching assistant for various courses offered at Johns Hopkins University:
highlights: Spring 2023--Fall 2023 - Robot Devices, Kinematics, Dynamics, and Control

My responsibilities include grading, holding office hours, and (for Spring 2022 and Fall 2022) running weekly lab sessions that guide  students through practical applications of the contents covered in class.

awards.title: Best Student Paper
awards.summary: My presentation of my paper 'Evaluation of a motion measurement system for PET imaging studies' won the Best Student Paper award, selected among all papers with student first-authors.

awards.title: Honorable mention
awards.summary: This award recognizes undergraduate students who show outstanding research potential in an area of computing research.

awards.title: Charles A. Miller Award
awards.awarder: Johns Hopkins University, Mechanical Engineering Department
awards.summary: This award recognizes outstanding academic achievement of an undergraduate JHU student in Mechanical Engineering. (2/40)
